title: Propagate touched_files to shared followup beads before dispatch
description: When the scheduler activates a shared tester, review, or documentation bead (one with multiple developer bead dependencies), it must auto-populate the bead's touched_files by aggregating touched_files and changed_files from the handoff summaries of all completed dependencies before dispatching it to a worker. Without this, shared followup beads start with empty touched_files and review/tester agents correctly refuse to proceed. Implementation: in scheduler.py, just before a bead is dispatched to a worker (in _dispatch() or equivalent), check if it is a tester/review/documentation bead with multiple done dependencies. If so, collect touched_files and changed_files from each dependency's handoff_summary and merge them (deduplicated) into the bead's touched_files. Only apply to beads with multiple developer dependencies — leave standalone per-bead followups unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: Shared review/tester/docs beads have touched_files populated from dependency handoff summaries before dispatch
A shared followup bead never starts with empty touched_files when its dependencies have touched files

handoff_summary.completed: Updated `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` so shared followup beads with multiple completed developer dependencies merge `handoff_summary.touched_files` and `handoff_summary.changed_files` into the bead's `touched_files` before worker dispatch. Also mirrored the merged set into `changed_files` to keep downstream file-scope state aligned. Verified syntax with `uv run python -m py_compile src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py`.
handoff_summary.remaining: Acceptance criterion about running the existing test suite remains for the tester agent; no tests were run here per guardrails.
handoff_summary.risks: The new aggregation runs at dispatch time and only considers completed developer dependencies. If a shared followup depends on non-developer beads that also contribute relevant scope, they are intentionally ignored.

execution_history.summary: Added dispatch-time aggregation of dependency handoff file scope for shared tester/review/documentation beads.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
